---
title: "Deploying RAG in Bedrock vs. Local: WattBot 2025 Case Study"
date: 2026-02-17
author:
  - "Nils Matteson"
  - "Blaise Enuh"
  - "Chris Endemann"
image: "https://img.youtube.com/vi/WYSzI3WZmKo/maxresdefault.jpg"

language:
  title-block-author-single: "Presenter"
  title-block-author-plural: "Presenters"
  title-block-published: "Date"

categories:
  - Videos
  - ML+X
  - UW-Madison
  - RAG
  - Retrieval
  - LLM
  - Cloud
  - AWS
  - Bedrock
  - Hugging Face
  - Foundation models
  - GenAI
  - Sustainability
  - Energy
  - Benchmarking
  - GPU
  - Deep learning
  - NLP
---

## About this resource
Many researchers are exploring retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to build document-grounded, trustworthy AI tools, but it is often unclear how design choices around models, infrastructure, and deployment play out in practice. In this session, we present lessons learned from replicating the winning RAG system from the WattBot 2025 challenge. The challenge focuses on producing citation-backed energy and sustainability estimates for AI workloads from a fixed corpus of 30+ academic papers — or explicitly abstaining when evidence is missing. After a short overview of the winning approach, **Nils Matteson** and **Blaise Enuh** walk through how the system is implemented in practice, including:

1. A cloud deployment using AWS Bedrock  
2. Local, open-source deployments (e.g., Hugging Face models on GB10 and Dell PowerEdge R7725 hardware)

The session compares performance, cost, latency, and operational tradeoffs across environments. It also includes a Streamlit-based interface demo for those looking to host their own RAG apps.

This work is conducted as part of ongoing AI infrastructure evaluation within the Research Cyberinfrastructure (RCI) office in DoIT at UW–Madison.

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WYSzI3WZmKo >}}

### Links
- **GitHub**: [WattBot in Bedrock and Local](https://github.com/matteso1/KohakuRAG_UI/)
- **Kaggle challenge**: [WattBot 2025](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/WattBot2025/overview)
- **Winning solution**: [KohakuBlueleaf/KohakuRAG](https://github.com/KohakuBlueleaf/KohakuRAG)
- [Machine Learning Marathon](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/): Learn about the annual Machine Learning Marathon (3-month AI/ML hackathon) hosted by ML+X each fall. Reach out to [Chris](mailto:endemann@wisc.edu) if you'd like to submit a project!

## See also
- [**Talk**: AI's Environmental Footprint: Insights and Actions](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Applications/Videos/Forums/mlx_2025-09-09.html): The previous ML+X forum where WattBot was first introduced, covering AI sustainability measurement and the RAG-based challenge design.
- [**Notebook**: Exploring Fact-Based QA with RAG: Romeo and Juliet](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Notebooks/2025-05-07_RAG-Romeo-Juliet.html): Learn how to build an end-to-end RAG pipeline from scratch using sentence-transformers and Hugging Face models.
- [**Workshop**: Intro to AWS SageMaker for Predictive ML/AI](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Amazon_SageMaker.html): Hands-on workshop covering AWS SageMaker and Bedrock for cloud-based ML/AI workflows, including RAG deployment.
- [**Resource**: UW Generative AI Services & Policies](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/GenAI/GenAI-at-UW-Madison.html): Overview of vetted GenAI tools at UW-Madison, including AWS Bedrock access.
